task.title: Exercises and labs (hands-on)
task.shortdesc: Five progressive labs that build on each other: callout note styling, step anchors with highlighting, custom meta injection, collapsible sidebar TOC, and full plug-in packaging with CI.

prereq.p: Before starting these labs, set up the exercise environment:

ol.li: Navigate to the exercises/ folder in this training repository (see exercises/README.md for full details).
Build the baseline output (no plug-in) to see default DITA-OT rendering: dita -i exercises/lab-sample.ditamap -f html5 -o exercises/out/html5 --nav-toc=full
Copy the starter plug-in into your DITA-OT install: cp -r exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs DITA-OT/plugins/ cd DITA-OT && dita --install

ul.li: exercises/topics/what-is-acmedoc.dita — concept with all 4 note types (Lab 1)
exercises/topics/install-acmedoc.dita — task with 5 steps and IDs (Lab 2)
exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/ — starter plug-in skeleton with commented templates
exercises/build/html5.properties — build properties for repeatable builds

context.p: Each lab builds on the previous one. Complete them in order to assemble a full production plug-in. All XSLT templates and CSS in the starter files are commented out — uncomment each section as you reach that lab.

info.p: Goal: Style <note> elements as colored callout boxes with icons.

ol.li: Open exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/xsl/html5/override.xsl and uncomment the Lab 1 section — the template matching *[contains(@class,' topic/note ')]:
Open exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/css/brand.css and uncomment the Lab 1 callout styles (.callout-note, .callout-warning, .callout-caution, .callout-tip).
Install the plug-in and build the sample map: dita --install dita -i exercises/lab-sample.ditamap -f html5 -o exercises/out/html5 --nav-toc=full
Open exercises/out/html5/what-is-acmedoc.html — this topic contains all 4 note types.

info.p: Expected result: Notes appear as styled boxes with color-coded left borders instead of plain paragraphs.

cmd.b: Lab 2: Step anchors with URL hash highlighting

info.p: Goal: Add id anchors to task steps so users can link directly to a step, with JS highlight on load.

ol.li: In exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/xsl/html5/override.xsl, uncomment the Lab 2 section — the template for task/step:
In exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/js/step-highlight.js, uncomment the entire script. It detects #step-N URL hashes, scrolls to the matching element, and adds a .highlighted CSS class.
In exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/css/brand.css, uncomment the Lab 2 step-highlight styles (.highlighted with yellow background and transition).
Rebuild and open exercises/out/html5/install-acmedoc.html#step-3 — this task has 5 steps, each with unique IDs like step-download, step-extract, etc.

info.p: Expected result: Navigating to page.html#step-3 scrolls to and highlights step 3.
Goal: Add a <meta name="generator" content="DITA-OT + com.extense.html5.labs"> tag to every page.

ol.li: In the DITA-OT source, locate the named template gen-user-head in plugins/org.dita.html5/xsl/topic.xsl — this empty template is called from chapterHead specifically for user customizations. Override it to inject meta tags.

Uncomment the Lab 3 section in exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/xsl/html5/override.xsl. The template overrides the empty gen-user-head and adds your custom meta tags directly — no xsl:next-match needed since the default template is empty: <xsl:template name="gen-user-head"> <meta name="generator" content="DITA-OT + com.extense.html5.labs"/> <meta name="dita-class" content="{normalize-space(/*/@class)}"/> </xsl:template>
Rebuild: dita --install dita -i exercises/lab-sample.ditamap -f html5 -o exercises/out/html5 --nav-toc=full
Open any output page and view source — confirm both custom meta tags appear in the <head>.

info.p: Expected result: Every HTML page contains your custom meta tags in the <head>.
Goal: Override the TOC to add CSS classes and make it a collapsible sidebar.

ol.li: Open exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/xsl/toc/toc-override.xsl and uncomment the template. It wraps the TOC in a <nav class="sidebar-toc"> with data-level attributes on each <li> for nested styling.
In exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/js/sidebar-toggle.js, uncomment the toggle script. It adds a hamburger button on viewports narrower than 768px.
Rebuild the sample map (which has 3 levels of nesting) and verify the sidebar renders correctly: dita --install dita -i exercises/lab-sample.ditamap -f html5 -o exercises/out/html5 --nav-toc=full

info.p: Expected result: A sticky, scrollable sidebar TOC with indented levels and a collapse toggle.

cmd.b: Lab 5: Full plug-in packaging and CI integration

info.p: Goal: Package all previous labs into a single plug-in, pin the toolkit version, and automate builds.

ol.li: Verify the complete plug-in structure in exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/: com.extense.html5.labs/ plugin.xml xsl/html5/override.xsl xsl/toc/toc-override.xsl css/brand.css js/step-highlight.js js/sidebar-toggle.js
Open exercises/plugins/com.extense.html5.labs/plugin.xml — confirm it declares both extension points (dita.xsl.html5 and dita.xsl.html5.toc).

Ensure all Lab 1–4 code sections are uncommented, then install and build: dita --install dita -i exercises/lab-sample.ditamap -f html5 -o exercises/out/html5 \ --args.cssroot=css --args.css=brand.css --args.copycss=yes --nav-toc=full
Review the CI reference at exercises/build/sample-github-workflow.yml. Adapt it for your own repository (update paths, DITA-OT version pin, and output directory).
Test the upgrade path: install a newer DITA-OT version, rebuild, and diff the outputs.

info.p: Expected result: A portable, version-pinned plug-in that builds in CI and produces consistent output across upgrades.

p.b: Verification checklist — confirm each after completing the corresponding lab:

ol.li: Lab 1: Open what-is-acmedoc.html — all 4 note types (note, tip, warning, caution) render as colored callout boxes with distinct left borders.
Lab 2: Open install-acmedoc.html#step-3 — the page scrolls to step 3 and highlights it with a yellow background.
Lab 3: View source on any output page — <meta name="generator"> and <meta name="dita-class"> appear in the <head>.
Lab 4: Open any page — a sticky sidebar TOC with indented levels appears. On mobile widths (below 768px), a toggle button shows/hides it.
Lab 5: Push to a GitHub repo with the sample workflow — the CI build succeeds and publishes to gh-pages.
